name: Levada Trail
name_original: Rota da Levada (PR 3) e das Aldeias do Xisto (PR 2) — Serra da Lousã
region: Lousã e Vilarinho e Vilarinho e Vilarinho
description: This difficult 13.7-mile jogging route in Serra da Lousã combines the historic Levada PR3 and picturesque Schist Villages PR2.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lousã e Vilarinho e Vilarinho e Vilarinho traverse the Serra da Lousã, a mountainous region characterized by schist geology and dense forests. The landscape features deeply incised river valleys, leading to natural river pools and waterfalls. Elevations reach up to Alto do Trevim at 1,205 meters, offering varied terrain for running. Best jogging routes around Lousã e Vilarinho e Vilarinho e Vilarinho The most popular jogging route is Levada Trail, a 13.7 miles (22.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows watercourses through the mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Talasnal Schist Village – Lousã Castle loop from Lousã, a difficult 12.5 miles (20.1 km) path. This route connects historic schist villages with the medieval Lousã Castle. Local runners also love the Lousã Castle – Senhora da Piedade River Pool loop from Lousã, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical sites, often completed in about 51 minutes. Jogging around Lousã e Vilarinho e Vilarinho e Vilarinho is defined by the rugged Serra da Lousã, historic schist villages, and picturesque river valleys. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Lousã e Vilarinho e Vilarinho e Vilarinho are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. More than 390 runners have used komoot to explore Lousã e Vilarinho e Vilarinho e Vilarinho's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Lousã region?
A: The Lousã e Vilarinho region offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 30 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including both moderate and difficult options.

Q: What makes the Lousã region unique for trail running?
A: The Lousã region is unique for its dramatic mountainous landscapes within the &lt;strong&gt;Serra da Lousã&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by schist geology and dense forests. Runners can experience picturesque river valleys, stunning waterfalls, and charming historic &lt;strong&gt;Schist Villages&lt;/strong&gt;, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural immersion.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Lousã?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are 19 moderate routes available. These might be suitable for beginners looking for a less strenuous experience compared to the more difficult trails. Currently, there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the guide data.

Q: Are there long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, experienced runners will find several challenging long-distance trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2197568291/levada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Levada Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.1 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto do Trevim loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt; extends to nearly 26 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I combine my run with visiting historical sites or charming villages?
A: Absolutely! Many routes integrate the region's rich history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talasnal Schist Village – Lousã Castle loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt; connects historic schist villages like Talasnal with the medieval &lt;strong&gt;Castle of Lousã&lt;/strong&gt;. You can also explore other Schist Villages such as Cerdeira, Casal Novo, Chiqueiro, and Candal, which are often part of the trail network.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the jogging routes in Lousã?
A: Yes, the Lousã region is home to several beautiful waterfalls. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2890426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Ferida Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4006009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira de Espinho Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5579835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Cave in Serra da Lousã&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10370364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascata da Lousã loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that passes by a waterfall.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints to enjoy during a run?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain offers breathtaking panoramic vistas. The &lt;strong&gt;Alto do Trevim&lt;/strong&gt;, the highest point of the Serra da Lousã, provides spectacular views. Other viewpoints like the 'This is Lousã' viewpoint also offer great spots to pause and take in the scenery.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging trails in Lousã e Vilarinho?
A: The running routes in Lousã e Vilarinho are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the integration of historic sites, and the natural beauty of the Serra da Lousã.

Q: Are the jogging routes in Lousã typically circular?
A: Many of the popular running routes in Lousã are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talasnal Schist Village – Lousã Castle loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21948914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lousã Castle – Senhora da Piedade River Pool loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Lousã?
A: While many natural trails in Portugal are generally dog-friendly, it's always advisable to check local regulations or specific trail signage. The region's extensive network of forest trails and mountain paths often allows for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near the starting points of many popular trails, especially in and around Lousã town and the larger Schist Villages. It's recommended to check specific route details or local signage for designated parking areas.

Q: Are there places to refuel or get refreshments near the running paths?
A: Yes, the charming &lt;strong&gt;Schist Villages&lt;/strong&gt; often have small cafes or taverns where you can find refreshments. Lousã town itself also offers various options for food and drink. Some river beaches, like &lt;strong&gt;Praia Fluvial da Senhora da Piedade&lt;/strong&gt;, may also have facilities.
tips:
Hidden among pine forests, the village of Chiqueiro seems suspended in time. With just a couple of inhabitants and their large flock, it maintains a simplicity that nature embraces. 

Two steep streets wind between the dark slate houses, rustic and without plaster, except for the small chapel.

The castle, built in the second half of the 11th century, is located in the Serra da Lousã, on the right bank of the Arouce River. Free entry, easy and free parking. Great hiking trails nearby (PR3 Rota da Levada).

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for casual jogging?
faqs.answer: No, this route is generally not recommended for casual jogging. Both the Route of the Levada (PR3) and the Schist Villages (PR2) sections are rated as difficult due to steep gradients, narrow and potentially slippery paths, significant elevation changes, and uneven terrain. It is more suited for experienced hikers or trail runners who are well-prepared for technical mountain trails.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied and challenging terrain. This includes narrow paths, steep ascents and descents, walled paths, farming terraces, and sections with rocks. Parts of the trail run alongside a 'levada' (water canal), which can be narrow with potential drops. After rainfall, sections may be slippery and mossy, especially in forested areas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass through charming Schist Villages like Chiqueiro Village, Talasnal, and Casal Novo. Other highlights include the historic Lousã Castle, the Ermida Hydroelectric Power Station (the first in mainland Portugal), ancient chestnut groves, and the Cruz Viewpoint offering panoramic views. You might also encounter the Ribeira de S. João, natural pools, and a waterfall near Talasnal.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails in the Serra da Lousã region. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Rota da Levada and the Rota das Aldeias do Xisto da Lousã. You may also find it intersecting with parts of the [CIMA PR09] Alto do Trevim * Lousa, Rota do Trevim, and other local paths like the Levada de Água.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: The Serra da Lousã region is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for hiking or trail running, with milder temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder, wetter conditions, making some sections more challenging due to mud and slippery surfaces.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Levada (PR 3) and of the Schist Villages (PR 2) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on these trails are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in the Serra da Lousã. However, given the challenging terrain, narrow paths, and potential for wildlife encounters, it is highly recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are accustomed to strenuous mountain trails.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Serra da Lousã park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Serra da Lousã region, including the Route of the Levada (PR3) and the Schist Villages (PR2). The area is part of the Natura 2000 Network, emphasizing conservation, but remains freely accessible for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailheads?
faqs.answer: Access points for these routes are often near the Schist Villages themselves or the Lousã Castle. For the PR2 (Schist Villages), many start near the castle or villages like Talasnal. For the PR3 (Levada), it connects Cerdeira to Lousã Castle. Parking is usually available in or near these villages, though it might be limited during peak times.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: The Serra da Lousã is rich in biodiversity and part of the Natura 2000 Network. While jogging, you might have the chance to spot various wildlife, including deer, wild boar, and roe deer, especially during quieter times of the day.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate gear. This includes sturdy hiking boots with good grip, sufficient water (as availability on the trail may be limited), snacks, a first-aid kit, and weather-appropriate clothing. A map or GPS device is also advisable, as well as trekking poles for stability on steep or slippery sections.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Schist Villages themselves, such as Talasnal, Chiqueiro Village, and Casal Novo, often have small cafes or traditional restaurants where you can find refreshments and local cuisine. The town of Lousã, where the Lousã Castle is located, also offers more dining options.
